text:
The Special Olympics Colorado State Summer Games will begin at 7:30 p.m. tonight at Bergman Field on the Colorado Mesa University campus. But the support that fuels these games has been forthcoming for many years.

Law enforcement agencies across the country have supported the Special Olympics through a program called the Law Enforcement Torch Run since 1981. Various agencies host events each year tailored to their individual communities. The goal is to raise awareness and funds for the Special Olympics.

In the Grand Valley, the Mesa County Sheriff’s Office hosts the annual Glow N Games to raise money for the Special Olympics. The focal point of the Glow N Games is a 5K run after dark. Participants wear bright clothing and use color powder and black lights to glow as they run.

The event was more than a run with other activities available like lawn games, bouncy houses and food trucks. The goal is to create an event that is inclusive to the whole community

This year’s Glow N Games took place this on June 1 at Long Family Park. About 100 people raced in the 5K and the event raised more than $18,000 for the Special Olympics, more than in previous years.

“This year was a very successful year” said Lt. Wade Pacheco of the Sheriff’s Office.

Pacheco has been involved in organizing this event since it was started in 2017. In fact, the very next year, the Mesa County Sheriff’s Office received the Agency of the Year Award. In 2022, the MCSO was inducted into the Special Olympics Hall of Fame for its work, fundraising and commitment to Special Olympics.

Image byJill HeyerfromPixabay

These awards demonstrate MCSO’s commitment to give back to the community.

“It’s really a privilege to be able to work with the athletes and cheer them on and be a part of their celebration when they receive their medals,” Pacheco said.

The Glow N Games was a successful event, but that’s not the only event that the MCSO will be hosting this year. Beginning at 7:30 a.m. this morning — until supplies run out — the Sheriff’s Office will be selling donuts to celebrate National Donut Day. All of the proceeds will go to the Special Olympics.

Anyone who wants to purchase donuts can get them drive-thru style from the Sheriff’s Office at 215 Rice St.. This event “plays on the cops and donuts” stereotype for a good cause, Pacheco said.

Other events include the “tip-a-cop” fundraiser hosted by law enforcement agencies across the Grand Valley. The Sheriff’s Office has worked with various police departments to raise money at restaurants by assisting waitstaff, bussing tables and serving food.

This event will take place in the coming months.

This weekend, law enforcement agents from around the state will attend the State Summer Games to support athletes and hand out awards.

Anyone who is able to volunteer at this event is encouraged to sign-up through the Special Olympics Colorado website athttps://specialolympicsco.org/event/statesummergames/
